artPrompt: A characterful portrait of a wiry goblin rogue darting through a rain-slick alley, a fistful of coins clutched to his chest, counting on his clawed fingers with a furrowed look of slow concentration, a cap pulled low. Amber lamplight catches falling raindrops and the glint of his cut. Shadowy figures gamble at a card table in the background. Mono-black noir of ink shadow and smoky brown, twitchy criminal hustle, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Numbers Runner Goblin
text: Whenever a player rolls one or more dice or flips one or more coins, you may pay {1}. If you do, you create a Favor token. The Runner takes a cut of every action in town.
flavorText: He runs the numbers for the whole carnival. Slowly. On his fingers.
